name: Rod Stewart
abstract: Sir Roderick David Stewart (born 10 January 1945) is a British singer and songwriter. Known for his distinctive raspy singing voice, Stewart is among the best-selling music artists of all time, having sold more than 120 million records worldwide.

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I played a gig a couple of months ago and some woman said to me, “We want to hear those older songs, those Rod Stewart songs.” [INTERVIEWEE] Right. There’s another one. I didn’t mention him, should have.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: David Finck
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve gotten a chance to record a lot of pop music, people like Rod Stewart... Like those “Rod Stewart Sings Standards” records... He was on one or two sessions that we did in Los Angeles.
about_quotes.doc_title: David Finck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, May 29, 2015

about_quotes.speaker: John Altman
about_quotes.evidence: So for example an interesting one in terms of the pop music was Rod Stewart’s version of “Downtown Train,” the Tom Waits song, because we recorded it in the key of G
about_quotes.doc_title: John Altman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, September 14, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Plas Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I was just called... that may not be my solo on Rod Stewart’s record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Plas Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996
